serviceDescription: Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus is a Prometheus-compatible monitoring service that makes it easy to monitor containerized applications at scale. The Cloud Native Computing Foundation’s Prometheus project is a popular open source monitoring and alerting solution optimized for container environments. With Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus, you can use the open source Prometheus query language (PromQL) to monitor the performance of containerized workloads, without having to manage the underlying infrastructure.

templates.title: Calculate the cost using your existing self-hosted Prometheus

cards.title: Metric sample ingestion
cards.helpText: Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus counts each metric sample ingested to the secured Prometheus-compatible endpoint.

components.label: Average active time series
components.description: Run prometheus_tsdb_head_series in your local prometheus

allRegions.meteredUnit: Metric from 40000000 to 2000000000 per Metric Datapoints
Metric from 2000000000 to 252000000000 per Metric Datapoints
Metric from 252000000000 to Inf per Metric Datapoints

cards.title: Storage
cards.helpText: Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus calculates the stored metric samples and metric metadata in gigabytes (GB), where 1GB is 2^30 bytes. The storage charge is determined by the Prometheus metrics samples (typically 1 or 2 bytes) and metadata.

meteredUnit.allRegions: Storage from 10 to Inf per GB-Mo

cards.title: Query Samples Processed
cards.helpText: Amazon Managed Service for Prometheus meters the Query Samples Processed (QSP) from all QueryMetric API requests. QSP is the total number of data points queried using Prometheus Query Language (PromQL) for a given time range.
